title: Wedelia thouarsii
taxonomy: Wedelia thouarsii is classified as a member of the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is due to its shared characteristics with other plants in the family, such as having composite flowers and a similar leaf structure. The genus Wedelia itself is a group of flowering plants native to tropical regions, and Wedelia thouarsii is one of the species within this genus. The species is further classified as a perennial herb, growing in various parts of the world. Wedelia thouarsii is a member of the tribe Heliantheae, which includes other plants known for their showy flowers and daisy-like appearance.

intro: Wedelia thouarsii, also known as Wedelia, is a flowering plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Caribbean and Central America, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Wedelia thouarsii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Wedelia is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, or in the form of a tincture or capsule, to take advantage of its therapeutic potential.
